steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both texts must share a central topic. Text 1 describes why sloths are hard to study — they live high in trees. Text 2 describes how scientists use monitors to discover sloth behaviors. The shared focus is scientists grappling with understanding sloths.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Scientists' efforts to understand sloths: covers both texts — Text 1 explains the difficulty, Text 2 explains the response. ✓
  • Choice B — Changes in sloths' habitats: too broad and not Text 1's focus — Text 1 doesn't discuss technology. ✗
  • Choice C — How extinct sloths compare with living sloths: only Text 1's detail; not addressed in Text 2. ✗
  • Choice D — The benefits of putting monitoring devices on sloths: only Text 2's angle; Text 1 doesn't discuss discoveries. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
